source_intent.raw_intent: Case 058: I am a nontechnical_business_owner in Africa. Build a sr9_orchestration_prompt for workflow_orchestration using the compliance_lens. Chaos input type is tool_permission_confusion. The source is intentionally messy: mix missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Block unsafe certainty and record a repair receipt. Return something SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract.

parsed_tester_json.blocked_reason: The raw intent contains ambiguous acceptance criteria, missing context (e.g., specific compliance framework, tool inventory, user roles), and urgent language that precludes safe orchestration. The compliance_lens requires explicit regulatory references and permission boundaries; tool_permission_confusion as chaos input demands a clear baseline of allowed vs. denied actions. Without these, any generated workflow would risk unauthorized access or non-compliance. A repair receipt is required before proceeding.
parsed_tester_json.compiled_intent: Objective: Produce an SR9 orchestration prompt that, when compiled, generates a workflow orchestrating compliance checks under tool_permission_confusion conditions, with a blocked-state receipt and repair instructions. Scope: - Single orchestration prompt for Codex runtime; must include compliance_lens, handle tool_permission_confusion as primary chaos vector, and output a repair receipt if blocked. Constraints: - Must not assume any specific compliance framework (e.g., GDPR, SOC2) without explicit mention. - Must not generate executable code or permissions without a baseline tool inventory and role definitions. - Must block if acceptance criteria are ambiguous or missing context that could lead to unsafe execution. - Must record a repair receipt detailing missing fields and required clarifications. Success Criteria: - SR8 compiler returns a valid SR9 orchestration prompt with blocked_state_receipt set to true. - Repair receipt includes at least: missing compliance framework, undefined tool permissions, ambiguous acceptance criteria, and required user roles. - No executable workflow is generated until repair is acknowledged. Output Contract: - artifact_type: sr9_orchestration_prompt - depth_tier: D3_SOVEREIGN_CORE - runtime_target: Codex - should_block: True - blocked_reason: String describing the specific missing/ambiguous elements that prevent safe compilation. - compiled_intent: Object with Objective, Scope, Constraints, Success Criteria, and Output Contract as defined above. - repair_receipt: missing_context: compliance_framework; tool_inventory; user_roles; ambiguous_fields: acceptance_criteria; permission_boundaries; urgent_language_flag: True; recommended_repair: Provide explicit compliance framework (e.g., 'GDPR Article 5'), list of tools and their allowed/denied permissions, and clear, testable acceptance criteria.
